text: Agile methodology is a project management approach often used in software development. Work is divided into short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two weeks. Each day, the team holds a quick standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. Before a sprint begins, the team selects tasks from a backlog, which is a prioritised list of work items. These tasks are called user stories, and they describe features from the perspective of the end-user. This process is part of iterative development, meaning the team regularly reviews and improves the product after each sprint. Agile helps teams adapt to changes quickly.
